body_txt: Did you know that there's an entire world outside of TV Tropes? Real Life is the space between works of fiction. It's where you and billions of other people eat, work, sleep, and live. Real Life and fiction live in a symbiotic relationship. Without Real Life, all the works we read and love would be meaningless, but fiction gives us the escapism we crave from reality. Real life is, well, real life — the physical world that you, I, and everyone else inhabits. It's the place where events happen because of "causality" and "historic causes" and "things just being that way" instead of using narrative like a sensible story ought to. Since we are a site dedicated to discussing tropes in narrative, gameplay, and worldbuilding in fiction, this doesn't always intersect directly with what we do, but its literal omnipresence means that we need to at least reference it from time to time. Also known as reality, existence, meatspace, and the place you have to go through when you need to leave the computer and go eat something, Real Life is that place that you visit when you're not flying around through time and space, questing to destroy cursed artifacts, dressing in colorful costumes to fight crime, and otherwise doing interesting things. Other notable traits of Real Life include being a notoriously inaccurate reflection of the true world of fiction. Because nothing that happens in real life is driven by narrative considerations, please do not add examples to this page , which simply defines this term. If you want to see examples of tropes that are more or less congruent to how they're depicted in fiction, see Truth in Television . Before adding examples to a trope's Real Life section, it is a good idea to read our Real Life Troping policy. As well, we have a page explaining our No Real Life Examples, Please! policy, as well as Limited Real Life Examples Only and No Recent Examples, Please! . Not to be confused with:
